import numpy as np
import seaborn as sns

import pandas as pd

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression


from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
from sklearn.datasets import load_digits

from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
from sklearn import metrics

import matplotlib.pyplot as matplot
import seaborn as sns

In [ ]:
df = [pd.read_csv("{}s.csv".format(decade)) for decade in ['60', '70', '80', '90', '2000', '2010']]
In [ ]:
for i, decade in enumerate([1960, 1970, 1980, 1990, 2000, 2010]):
    df[i]['decade'] = pd.Series(decade, index=df[i].index)

df = pd.concat(df, axis=0).sample(frac=1.0, random_state=1).reset_index(drop=True)

In [ ]:
scaled= scaler.fit_transform(X)
In [ ]:
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
wcss = []
for i in range(1,12):
  kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters = i, init = 'k-means++', max_iter = 300, n_init = 10, random_state = 0)
  kmeans.fit(scaled)
  wcss.append(kmeans.inertia_)
plt.plot(range(1,12), wcss, 'o')
plt.plot(range(1 , 12) , wcss , '-' , alpha = 0.5)
plt.title('Elbow Method - popular')
plt.xlabel('Number of Clusters')
plt.show()
In [ ]:
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters = 5, init = 'k-means++', max_iter = 300, n_init = 10, random_state = 0)
y_kmeans = kmeans.fit_predict(scaled)
In [ ]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13,11))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111)
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 0,0],scaled[y_kmeans == 0,1], s= 50, c= 'red',label= 'Cluster 1')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 1,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 1,1], s= 50, c= 'blue', label= 'Cluster 2')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 2,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 2,1], s= 50, c= 'green', label= 'Cluster 3')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 3,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 3,1], s= 50, c= 'cyan', label= 'Cluster 4')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 4,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 4,1], s= 50, c= 'magenta', label= 'Cluster 5')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 5,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 5,1], s= 50, c= 'yellow', label= 'Cluster 6')





plt.title('Clusters-popular')
plt.legend()

plt.show()
In [ ]:
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13,11))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 0,0],scaled[y_kmeans == 0,1], s= 50, c= 'red',label= 'Cluster 1')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 1,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 1,1], s= 50, c= 'blue', label= 'Cluster 2')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 2,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 2,1], s= 50, c= 'green', label= 'Cluster 3')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 3,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 3,1], s= 50, c= 'cyan', label= 'Cluster 4')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 4,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 4,1], s= 50, c= 'magenta', label= 'Cluster 5')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 5,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 5,1], s= 50, c= 'yellow', label= 'Cluster 6')


plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 5,0], scaled[y_kmeans=5,1],s = 50, c = 'purple', label = 'Cluster 7')
plt.scatter(scaled[y_kmeans == 7,0], scaled[y_kmeans == 7,1])



plt.title('Clusters-popular')
plt.legend()

plt.show()
In [ ]:
scaled_notpop = pd.DataFrame(scaler.fit_transform(Y))
scaled_notpop.columns = cols
In [ ]:
wcss = []
for i in range(1,12):
  kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters = i, init = 'k-means++', max_iter = 300, n_init = 10, random_state = 0)
  kmeans.fit(scaled_notpop)
  wcss.append(kmeans.inertia_)
plt.plot(range(1,12), wcss, 'o')
plt.plot(range(1 , 12) , wcss , '-' , alpha = 0.5)
plt.title('Elbow Method - not popular')
plt.xlabel('Number of Clusters')
plt.show()
In [ ]:
kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters = 3, init = 'k-means++', max_iter = 300, n_init = 10, random_state = 0)
y_kmeans = kmeans.fit_predict(scaled_notpop)
In [ ]:
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13,11))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
plt.scatter(scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 0,0],scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 0,1], s= 50, c= 'red',label= 'Cluster 1')
plt.scatter(scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 1,0], scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 1,1], s= 50, c= 'blue', label= 'Cluster 2')
plt.scatter(scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 2,0], scaled_notpop[y_kmeans == 2,1], s= 50, c= 'green', label= 'Cluster 3')








plt.title('Clusters - not popular')
plt.legend()

plt.show()
